title: heracleum sphondylium var. lanatum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium var. lanatum, also known as wild angelica, is a biennial or perennial plant in the carrot family, native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of wild angelica include reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
